programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer afternoon in upscale Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl is abducted from her home. Scores of police officers and FBI agents converge, determined to find the young girl before it is too late. When the kidnapper's car is discovered in a field near Lodi, police dogs, helicopters, and hundreds of volunteers are employed in a round-the-clock search. It would take a community united in concern for one of their youngest to find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer night in 1990, a body was discovered burning in a Minnesota alley. Weeks later, police finally identified the victim, a suspected drug courier from California. The FBI began working with local police and slowly uncovered a multi-state crime ring run by a deadly drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him silent. Agents needed to infiltrate and dismantle the crew before more people died.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By April of 1995 in Washington, D.C., two officers had been shot and one had been murdered. Each had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man sneaked up and opened fire. The FBI was brought in to help catch the cop killer. Agents identified the man who was most likely the killer, but he was nowhere to be found. Months of surveillance operations around the country finally yielded a break; they found their suspect in Baltimore, MD.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a California man was found dismembered in 1990, police suspected a local burglar, but they couldn't find him. Six months later, he was caught robbing an Arizona bank and sentenced to life. But prison couldn't hold him for long, and within a year he escaped. The FBI would lead a multi-agency manhunt for a killer on a desperate run through the tourist regions of the west.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 80's, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation. Regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. Desperate to stop the violence, local authorities turned to the FBI for help. One dealer bowed to no one. He would stop at nothing to keep detectives and agents from building a case against him. When his organization began targeting the government itself, a city went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, the decapitation of a young African-American male led the Miami-Dade police department and the FBI along a trail of murder. Investigators quickly discovered that the victim was a dissident member of a religious sect headed by a man calling himself Yahweh ben Yahweh, or God, Son of God. Although the FBI believed Yahweh ben Yahweh and his followers were responsible for many murders, they could find no direct evidence linking them to the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of becoming a doctor. But a tragic twist of fate intervened, when she found herself an innocent victim of the drug trade. Kidnapped and held captive, she survived for days against all odds. Authorities arrested her captors, but they refused to reveal her whereabouts. The FBI and local police raced against time, determined to save the teenager's life. Their investigation revealed a complex story of drugs, betrayal and revenge.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990's drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods. When a 10-year-old boy fell victim in the crossfire, NYPD detectives and FBI agents clamped down on the problem. They formed a task force called C-11, designed to dismantle the gangs. In an environment of violence and fear it would be difficult to identify, arrest and convict those involved in the secretive and deadly world of big money drugs.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He wandered the streets of New York City wearing only pajamas and a bathrobe, but the FBI was convinced that Vince Gigante, the reputed boss of the Genovese crime family, was anything but crazy. The FBI used undercover surveillance, secret audio tapes, six mobsters turned government informants and the testimony of twelve FBI agents to help convict Gigante of murder and conspiracy-to-murder, including a plot to kill rival mob boss John Gotti.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On June 21, 1964, three civil rights workers, two white and one black disappeared in Mississippi while passing through Neshoba County. A massive FBI investigation led to the recovery of the bodies of the slain men and physical evidence was recovered and analyzed. The FBI labs confirmed that all but one of the bullets used to kill the men came from the same gun. The FBI gained the trust of an informant, whose information led to the conviction of seven local Ku Klux Klan members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a young boy and his mother were kidnapped and murdered by a vicious drug cartel, FBI agents in the field worked tirelessly to pierce the secretive drug underworld and unearth those involved. Their surveillance and investigative work uncovered a violent trail of murder and greed. FBI agents and forensic examiners pieced together the evidence needed to draw out and then crush the cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In rural Texas, 13-year-old is kidnapped on her way to school. Frantic to find her safely, her millionaire father calls local authorities and the FBI. As police officers and agents conduct aerial surveillance, the father drives hundreds of miles through the night to deliver the ransom cash to a drop that the kidnappers keep changing. In a final effort to save the young girl, investigators must engage in a dangerous firefight with five desperate criminals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a Delaware woman vanished in 1996, family and friends discovered her secret life. The appointments secretary to the Governor had carried on an illicit affair with a wealthy married man. Agents suspected that her powerful ex-lover was responsible, but they had little physical evidence to support their theory. Investigators never found the woman's body or the murder weapon. The FBI's only hope was to go undercover, and turn brother against brother.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Corruption in the New Orleans Police Department grew like a cancer in the early 1990's. Lured by the easy wealth of the narcotics industry, crooked cops began breaking the laws they were sworn to uphold. They would stop at nothing, including murder, to protect their rackets. When it became clear they could no longer police themselves, the FBI got involved. Putting their lives on the line, undercover agents devised a way to flush out the violent cops and restore trust in the city's police force.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. They soon realized that Dhinsa was more than a crooked businessman, he enforced his mob-like operation with brutal violence, kidnapping and killing errant employees and any family members who questioned their deaths. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Racial tensions in Buffalo in 1980 were at an all-time high. Six black males had been brutally murdered--two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets. Residents screamed for the police to stop this serial killer. With no suspects, local investigators turned to the FBI. Profilers were able to produce a psychological composite of the type of person the killer would most likely be. Their analysis paid off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, the FBI began a massive undercover operation, code named Safebet, that was designed to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. Undercover agents quickly discovered that the mob had its tentacles spread into every facet of the law enforcement community. They unwittingly uncovered the murder of a wealthy socialite perpetrated by a jealous and greedy mob lawyer and covered up by the local police chief.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer afternoon in upscale Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl is abducted from her home. Scores of police officers and FBI agents converge, determined to find the young girl before it is too late. When the kidnapper's car is discovered in a field near Lodi, police dogs, helicopters, and hundreds of volunteers are employed in a round-the-clock search. It would take a community united in concern for one of their youngest to find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an eight-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood, leaving behind an abandoned bicycle and a frantic mother. Hundreds of citizens and police officers fanned out across the region, hoping to find some trace of the young girl or her abductor. Within hours, a suspect emerged; a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children. He was nowhere to be found and time had run out for the innocent child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Labor Day weekend turned into a nightmare of murder as a cross-country crime spree terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenage boy disappeared within hours of each other. Police believed the cases were linked, but didn't know how. They contacted the FBI for help. A trail of stolen vehicles and murder victims across seven states led agents on a hunt for two suspected killers. Their only hope of stopping them was to anticipate the killers' next move.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, south Miami became the home for a sadistic serial killer. Posing as a fashion photographer, Christopher Wilder lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead. As the FBI closed in around him, Wilder took off on a cross-country murderous rampage. The FBI added Wilder to their Most Wanted Fugitives program. The chase was on, and Wilder ultimately lost.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Larry Horn nearly got away with the perfect crime: a multiple murder-for-hire plot that was meticulously planned and executed. Horn's former wife, their 8-year-old son, and a live-in nurse were all brutally murdered by an assassin. In the absence of any significant clues, police in Maryland contacted the FBI to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El primero de octubre de 1993, un extraño secuestró a Polly Klaas de doce años durante una piyamada en su casa. El FBI de inmediato entró en el caso. Los agentes reunieron fibras de su habitación y descubrieron, a través del uso de fuentes de luz láser, la huella de una palma en la cama de Polly, previamente invisible al ojo humano. Después de una extensa investigación de dos meses, el FBI arrestó a Richard Allen Davis. La impresión de la palma y las fibras coincidieron y Davis fue condenado por secuestro y asesinato.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990's drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods. When a 10-year-old boy fell victim in the crossfire, NYPD detectives and FBI agents clamped down on the problem. They formed a task force called C-11, designed to dismantle the gangs. In an environment of violence and fear it would be difficult to identify, arrest and convict those involved in the secretive and deadly world of big money drugs.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He wandered the streets of New York City wearing only pajamas and a bathrobe, but the FBI was convinced that Vince Gigante, the reputed boss of the Genovese crime family, was anything but crazy. The FBI used undercover surveillance, secret audio tapes, six mobsters turned government informants and the testimony of twelve FBI agents to help convict Gigante of murder and conspiracy-to-murder, including a plot to kill rival mob boss John Gotti.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Kokomo, Indiana an unthinkable crime terrified residents. A young woman was snatched from her home on a bright spring morning. Drops of blood and a torn window screen were all that was left to tell authorities what happened to the twenty-one year old victim. After several days of searching, a former FBI profiler narrowed the suspect list to one. Agents and detectives followed the trail across state lines hoping to find the victim before her time ran out.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer night in 1990, a body was discovered burning in a Minnesota alley. Weeks later, police finally identified the victim, a suspected drug courier from California. The FBI began working with local police and slowly uncovered a multi-state crime ring run by a deadly drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him silent. Agents needed to infiltrate and dismantle the crew before more people died.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer afternoon in upscale Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl is abducted from her home. Scores of police officers and FBI agents converge, determined to find the young girl before it is too late. When the kidnapper's car is discovered in a field near Lodi, police dogs, helicopters, and hundreds of volunteers are employed in a round-the-clock search. It would take a community united in concern for one of their youngest to find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June of 1992, masked bandits pulled off a daring mid-day bank robbery. It marked the beginning of a four-year crime wave that rocked Seattle. With each new robbery, the bandits perfected their craft, leaving behind no evidence and escaping with hundreds and thousands of dollars. As the robbers became increasingly violent, pressure mounted to end their reign. With few leads, the FBI forced their own luck. The effort put them face to face with an armed and desperate fugitive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A day that began with a class field trip ended with a parent's worst nightmare. An 11-year-old girl was missing. Her parents were certain she was no runaway. When they failed to find her in their quiet Illinois neighborhood, they called the police. The FBI soon joined the investigation and family and friends worked with detectives and agents in an urgent search for the missing girl. Faced with conflicting witness reports and false leads, agents wouldn't stop until they found the girl, and the truth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A California woman's two former husbands are mysteriously murdered, nearly 20 years apart.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while, and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter who'd been shot three times was the victim of a well planned crime. Investigators suspected two weapons and two killers. But analysis of the forensic evidence pointed them in a different direction--one much closer to home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters had battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the southern California fires to several fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes would indicate that this was no accident. It was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman disappeared without a trace, there were two possible explanations: kidnapping or murder. Careful investigation, a forensic artist, and DNA profiling enabled police to find a single suspect, who was known to prey upon vulnerable women.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hikers near Anchorage, Alaska discovered a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Clinging to the sheet, they also discovered a tuft of red carpet fibers _ threads of evidence which led them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-man shrimp boat sank when a fast-moving, violent storm struck the Louisiana coast. The captain drowned, and the deckhand was rescued fourteen hours later. The deckhand was considered a hero until the captain's body was found.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon went missing, it appeared to be foul play. In his will, he left instructions regarding what was to happen if he died under violent circumstances_which were carried out after a hiker came across a bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of an American tourist in a Mexican resort focused law enforcement resources on both sides of the border. At first glance, the motive appeared to be robbery, but careful analysis of the forensic evidence pointed to something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation of the kidnapping of a prominent banker's wife led police on a wild goose chase. An unexpected discovery made by an entomologist led to a new suspect, and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water. The husband, however, had no recollection of the crime. A jury would have to decide between evidence and the mysteries of the mind.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was 1957, and California police were searching for a man who had committed several crimes in one night, including murder. They followed thousands of leads but eventually the case turned cold. Almost 50 years later, with the help of advances in computer technology and handwriting analysis, investigators proved an old adage: You can run, but you can't hide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an elderly couple died in a suspicious house fire, their son became the prime suspect. Investigators turned to forensic science to determine if the fire had been set deliberately, or if it was an unfortunate accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters found a skull in a Texas canyon, police found bits of clothing, small bones and a strand of hair. A forensic artist reconstructed her face. Now all they had to do was find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing, and police searched every square inch of the island; they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two young children was found dead in her bedroom. Her death was ruled a suicide _ but when investigators learned she had almost died in a house fire three years earlier, they decided to take another look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, when the wife of a serviceman was brutally murdered in the Philippines, investigators had to reassemble a 5-1/4 inch computer disk which had been cut to pieces with pinking shears in order to find the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer was found dead in her bed with a plastic laundry bag near her face. Further investigation proved that the scene had been staged. Her death wasn't an accident; it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A patrol officer is shocked by the amount of blood at a traffic accident and is shocked by the coroner's ruling until an anonymous phone call re-ignites the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The decomposed body of a young woman was discovered in a Bakersfield irrigation canal. If there was trace evidence, it had been washed away.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A behavioral profile caused the Baton Rouge Police Department to search for the wrong man. They might not have made an arrest had it not been for a DNA picture of the suspect painted by a molecular biologist.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Modesto, California teenager went missing. There was no sign of a struggle in her home, and police suspected she'd simply run away until her naked, bruised body was discovered in a ditch 20 miles away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police recovered the submerged car of a man reported missing, they expected to find his body _ but it wasn't there. The investigation which followed would uncover an obsession turned deadly, and the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Emergency dispatch received a call from a man who said his girlfriend shot and killed herself. The autopsy revealed that the gunshot wound was not self-inflicted and the evidence on her body would give police a golden opportunity to catch her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An employee of a dry cleaner was raped and murdered in the store. Police turned to forensic science for the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of an eccentric millionaire was not entirely unexpected; he flaunted his wealth and cared little for personal security. The evidence at the crime seemed to indicate robbery, but investigators wondered if there was something more.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes. A pair of bloody gloves, unique tire tracks, and ordinary grass and pine needles provided investigators with some extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensics hopes to determine if this was revenge when the home of a state's witness is bombed, killing his father.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb of Philadelphia, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning. The investigation gradually focused on one person, a suspect who had more than a million reasons to want the victim dead.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman was found dead on a golf course in the Bahamas. The grass on that course was so distinctive, it had evidentiary value. The evidence led police to two suspects. Each blamed the other, and they had to find out who the killer was.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind _ tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a popular disc jockey was found murdered in a community garden, police swung into action. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert led police to the killer and he'd been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down in his own garage, police discover that a wound sustained by the grieving widow may have been self-inflicted. They turn to science to help unravel a twisted tale of lust, greed and deception.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer had been careful _ he used poison which had no taste or odor. Fortunately for investigators, it also had a unique chemical signature.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a victim is brutally murdered in his own bed, investigators find a shoe impression in the mud outside--physical evidence they hope will lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist was on the loose in Washington, DC. Each of the fires was started with the same type of incendiary device. It was up to forensic scientists to find the clues that lay in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman was killed instantly when a bomb exploded in her home. The device was so powerful that shrapnel was embedded in houses across the street. The bomber had not only knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman could have lost control of her SUV on the icy road, and plunged down an embankment into a shallow creek. But there were footprints in the snow leading away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record, and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double-homicide.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a little girl got sick and died, investigators were stumped. Was it an accident, an unexplained illness, or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a car was found in a drainage ditch with two bodies inside, a fingertip torn from a latex glove would point investigators to both the crime scene and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A World War II veteran was found dead in his home, and the investigation ground to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots and had a sweet tooth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who was known to have suffered from depression apparently took her own life. But her sister told police that, a year before her death, she said if anything were to happen to her, there was a note in the china cabinet.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An aspiring model turned up dead, and the prime suspect was her boyfriend. When he was eventually cleared, investigators had to dig deeper to find the perpetrator. With the help of a forensic geologist, they identified the most unlikely suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One warm summer afternoon, the town of Verona, Wisconsin faced its first triple homicide. To solve the case, investigators had to delve into the world of high rollers and offshore betting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One warm summer afternoon, the town of Verona, Wisconsin faced its first triple homicide. To solve the case, investigators had to delve into the world of high rollers and offshore betting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a young girl was discovered on isolated farmland near Delano, California. She had no ID, but police found mailbox keys in the pocket of her jeans. With no other clues, they checked the mailboxes of every apartment building in Delano.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian on a Harrisburg street. The Jeep Grand Cherokee he was leasing around that time had been sold months ago to a buyer in another state.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled the death an accident, but the detectives investigating the case thought the evidence at the scene indicated otherwise.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run boating accident caused the death of a popular young man, investigators faced the daunting task of searching for one boat among 1200 others.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value, and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case, much less catch a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young man was killed in a mysterious car crash, but the evidence at the scene led investigators to believe it was not an accident. Forensic science revealed what really happened, and the truth devastated three families.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, 82-year-old Kathryn Bishop was found dead in her Pennsylvania home. Tiny clues on the victim's body would tell police what happened that night.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe. To solve the crime, police would have to find out who knew about the concealed safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The bomb was constructed to cause as much damage as possible... and it did, killing the victim with deadly force and flame. A painstaking search yielded tiny clues, which identified the killer as surely as if he'd signed them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A twelve-year-old girl claimed she had been abducted and sexually assaulted. She recounted what happened in such a flat, unemotional voice that police found it difficult to believe her. Fibers on her clothing would prove she was telling the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the number and volume of bloodstains at the scene was greater than usual, it was up to forensic scientists to find out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, and then a newly formed Cold Case Unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue _ a flaw on a mailing envelope _ exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, 19-year-old Shannon Melendi disappeared while at Emory University. For ten years, her disappearance remained a mystery, until new scientific testing cast a different light on Colvin 'Butch' Hinton.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, the death of Crystal Purcell was considered an accident. Then in 2001, Barbara Purcell called police to suggest that her estranged husband Willard had killed Crystal, who was her husband at the time of her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, Paul Gruber's daughter suspected something was wrong when she received birthday cards addressed from Gruber, on which the handwriting wasn't Gruber's.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman was dead. The man had been shot four times and survived. When the man's version of events was at odds with the evidence, investigators turned to forensic science, hoping to determine if this was a botched robbery, or cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers, and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After inspecting storm damage to a home in Tampa, the insurance assessor simply disappeared. Thirty hours later, her body was found in a nearby river. But the killer had been careless.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder trail turned cold, until police got a call from a woman whose husband, Gerald Powers, had a criminal past and a fondness for Chevy Berettas.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Grand Junction, Colorado is on edge after a series of deadly bombings which has killed 2 and injured one more. Police race to find the culprit before he strikes again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lives changed in the 20 years following an unsolved murder, and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed not only a clue no one had seen before but also the identity of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A lifelong resident of the tiny town of Lefroy, Tasmania was murdered outside his own home. Robbery appeared to be the motive, but with no suspects, the investigation came to a halt.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy socialite died after falling down the stairs, witnesses said one thing and evidence indicated another. To find out what really happened, investigators turned to forensic science, a physicist and an expert in accident reconstruction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Kelly Eckart's car was found with her belongings still in it, after working the late shift in Franklin, Indiana. Days later, her body was found in an isolated ravine.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A millionaire and his family were executed in their own home. For three years, the murders went unsolved and then a 30-year-old box of ammunition and some fluorescent fibers revealed the ultimate betrayal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was one of the most brazen crimes of the 20th century.¾Adolph Coors III, chairman of the Coors Brewing Company, was kidnapped and held for ransom prompting one of the most intense manhunts in United States history.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The development of technology could help solve a murder case that is twelve years old.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young girl was found dead, police quickly arrested the most likely suspect. But cutting-edge technology from NASA enabled a forensic odonatologist to prove the wrong man was behind bars.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It's usually easy to determine how a criminal entered the crime scene. But in this case, it was far from clear. It looked like the killer vanished into thin air...and perhaps he had.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young fireman died from what appeared to be serious but undiagnosed heart disease, his family and friends were devastated but they had no proof of foul play.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Security cameras in a casino tracked a young woman's movements until shortly before she disappeared. She was never seen again, but through the evidence she left behind, she was able to tell investigators what happened to her and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was one of the most unusual cases in forensic history. Investigators had to find a way to solve a murder case with evidence which consisted of a squashed tomato found at the crime scene, and tiny, pinpoint reflections of light in a photograph.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Seattle police had no suspects in the violent murder of post-grunge singer, Mia Zapata. More than a decade passed before the evidence collected by a prescient medical examiner could be used by forensic scientists to identify the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the 'dirt' on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How unlucky could one man be? His wife took her own life, and his college sweetheart killed herself in much the same fashion fourteen years earlier. Investigators had to determine if this was just a bizarre coincidence, or murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 54-year-old Gayle Isleib was ambushed in her Manchester, Connecticut driveway and shot to death. Now police must determine if love had turned into obsession... and a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had been sexually assaulted and stabbed to death on the beach. A pair of men's tennis shoes was discovered near her body. Police were sure that if they found the man who fit the shoes, they would also find the man who committed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home, and the only evidence¾the kidnapper¾left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial killer was on the loose and police had to find him before he struck again. Their most promising lead was an unusual one: a bloody fingerprint on the body of one of the victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On Halloween night 2004, Adriane Insogna and Leslie Mazzara were brutally murdered in their Napa, California home.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the suspect was convicted of arson and murder, he maintained his innocence but was sentenced to 25 years in prison. He turned to the law books in the prison library and TV about forensic science and set out to prove his innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was brutally murdered in her home, and the only witnesses to the crime were the family dogs. An expert in canine behavior was convinced the killer knew both the victim and the animals, and he was determined find out what the dogs had seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The killer probably hoped to cover his tracks by staging the crime scene. But investigators saw through the attempt almost immediately, and turned to forensic science to learn what really happened that night.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl ran and hid when she saw her grandmother being beaten to death, but the man followed, beat and assaulted her. She said the assailant was her uncle, who was convicted. She recanted her testimony years later.

programme.desc: In 1984, the body of college co-ed Laura Salmon was found on a Middle Tennessee farm, covered with her own denim jeans as well as the jeans of the killer.¾

In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, Leaders goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: Hoy
programme.desc: Cuatro conductores enfrentan su cotidianidad y un conflicto transformador durante sus viajes, lidiando con la persistente guerra civil en El Salvador.

programme.title: Quisieron bajar al jefe
programme.desc: Un viejo cacique asesina al padre de uno de los narcotraficantes más poderosos de la zona e intenta culpar al grupo rival para crear una guerra entre ellos y así adueñarse de todo el territorio.

programme.title: Carga blanca
programme.desc: Un hombre se hace pasar por un policía corrupto para buscar a los asesinos de su padre sin darse cuenta de que el también pertenecía al mismo grupo.

programme.title: A ellas les gustan mayores
programme.desc: Ricardo está casado con Miranda, una bella mujer de afortunada posición social. Sin embargo, Ricardo la engaña con una mujer más joven que él.

programme.title: Acábame de matar
programme.desc: Un oficial de policía está desahuciado y espera la muerte con orgullo, por eso contrata a un hombre para que lo aniquile.

programme.title: ¿Quién mató al patrón?
programme.desc: Un detective llega a un pueblo aparentemente tranquilo. Pero, en su primera noche, se produce un asesinato durante una fiesta organizada por la familia más rica del pueblo.

programme.title: Mi hermana es un peligro
programme.desc: La hermana de Carlos lo visita para conocer a su cuñada, pero él descubre que ambas viven un tórrido romance y planea vengarse con un asesino.

programme.title: A 3 caídas
programme.desc: El legendario luchador Tinieblas Jr. va en busca de los secuestradores de su hija con la ayuda de sus amigos.

programme.title: Oscuro veneno
programme.desc: La seductora agente secreto Helena regresa para cumplir una nueva misión: debe infiltrarse en el círculo privado de un científico genial, de quien sospechan que ha desarrollado un virus altamente mortal y contagioso.

programme.title: El corrido de Santa Amalia
programme.desc: Un hombre bueno y trabajador, atormentado por los recuerdos de la infancia, lucha por un amor prohibido, aunque termine en tragedia.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Se lleva a cabo una recepción para celebrar la boda de Jacob y Raquel, y Lea sabe que Labán no permitirá la boda. Esaú decide una prueba muy dura para Elizaf: dejarlo solo en la montaña un día para sobrevivir. Mahalat piensa aprovechar esa prueba.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: Una influencer de la moda se arriesga para promocionar la venta privada de la tienda departamental. Hyun-ah se preocupa cada vez más por las finanzas de su familia.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: Jung-sun encuentra a Sung-joon y Hyun-ah solos en la oficina fuera del horario de trabajo. Mi-na recibe una oferta tentadora para avanzar en su carrera.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: La tienda departamental entra en guerra con una tienda competidora para conseguir un trato con una exclusiva marca de joyería. Jung-sun tiene problemas para perdonar.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: Seong-jun soporta hacer el trabajo sucio de Ha Jae-ung por un puesto en la cima. Jeong-seon tiende una trampa para atrapar a la amante de su marido.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: En la subasta, Mi-na trabaja el doble y Yu-ri es humillada. Ma Sang-u descubre un video revelador en el teléfono móvil de Seong-jun.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: El líder de los perros se desespera ya que, al parecer, nada ha salido como él esperaba. Akkiz y Batuga tienen que enfrentar a los perros con estrategia, ya que no cuentan con un gran ejército. Se prepara el enfrentamiento definitivo con los perros.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Alpagur regresa triunfante a la capital tras su enfrentamiento con los perros y anuncia la boda de Akkiz y Batuga. Los novios le piden a Alpagur que después de la boda se lleve a cabo el juicio. El líder de los perros está derrotado.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Se lleva a cabo la boda de Akkiz y Batuga, en una ceremonia que parecía imposible para los dos. Alpagu debe cumplir su promesa de hacer el juicio contra Ulu Ece y Kaya. Los perros planean un golpe definitivo al palacio del cielo.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Una explosión ejecutada por los perros y su líder cambia toda la historia de los turcos. El reino del cielo ahora tiene a los chinos como su enemigo principal. Los turcos están cerca de su extinción si no ponen fin a esta amenaza.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Tras derrotar a los perros gracias al apoyo de los chinos, estos le piden a Temur que sea el nuevo Khan. Temur no acepta y se desata una batalla campal en el palacio. La princesa china vuelve a arriesgarse por Temur a pesar de que él no la ama.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Los chinos han tomado el palacio y tienen preso a Batuga, que era la última esperanza de los turcos. Ulu Ece tendrá la oportunidad de redimirse por todas sus maldades. Akkiz está sola y cumplir con su destino parece una misión imposible.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Historia bíblica que sigue a Josué mientras lidera a los hebreos hacia Canaán. Josué enfrenta diversos desafíos, incluyendo la oposición de Samara, quien está enamorada de él.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Albieri es un científico dedicado a las clonaciones, es padrino de Diego y Lucas, hijos gemelos de su amigo Leonardo Ferrer. Lucas conoce a Jade, una joven musulmana criada en Estados Unidos.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade no comprende lo malo de sus actos, su tío Alí la reprende y la abofetea. Leonardo se enfurece por lo sucedido entre su novia Cristina y Diego, confronta a su hijo para que diga la verdad.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Cristina, bajo presión, busca a Diego para decirle que la deje en paz, pero él no baja la guardia, ella se desviste y lo besa. Él responde, pero luego la rechaza y ella lo denuncia.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Alí anuncia los matrimonios de sus sobrinas: Latiffa con Mohamed y Jade con Said. Latiffa reacciona mal y Jade, entre lágrimas, confiesa que está enamorada de otro. Tiene una cita con Lucas.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Rosa es devuelta con Sebastián. Serafina le cuenta a Violetita sobre su origen y que Dalia, su madre, quiere contactarla, la joven se rehúsa. Henrique busca consuelo en las cortesanas. Rosa recibe el mismo castigo que Helena.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Belchior cuida con devoción a Isaura. Leoncio trata de propasarse con Isaura, sin importarle el estado de su esclava. Álvaro comienza con síntomas de fiebre amarilla, pero él quiere regresar por Isaura.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: En medio de la enfermedad de Álvaro, Geraldo le informa a su amigo las intenciones de Leoncio para casar a Isaura con Belchior. Leoncio se pone violento con Isaura y Belchior intercede.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: En la subasta, Mi-na trabaja el doble y Yu-ri es humillada. Ma Sang-u descubre un video revelador en el teléfono móvil de Seong-jun.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Se lleva a cabo una recepción para celebrar la boda de Jacob y Raquel, y Lea sabe que Labán no permitirá la boda. Esaú decide una prueba muy dura para Elizaf: dejarlo solo en la montaña un día para sobrevivir. Mahalat piensa aprovechar esa prueba.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Besime busca a Melissa en casa de Metin. Melissa y Metin le piden a Mahinur que acepte quedarse con Melissa y así la pequeña podrá ir a la escuela. Ella prefiere regresar al orfanato que ir con Besime. Mahinur recibe una llamada.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Alí anuncia los matrimonios de sus sobrinas: Latiffa con Mohamed y Jade con Said. Latiffa reacciona mal y Jade, entre lágrimas, confiesa que está enamorada de otro. Tiene una cita con Lucas.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Manuel tiene argumento para creer que Efraín puede ser un sospechoso en caso del abuso de Valentina. Emilio pierde la cabeza y va con arma a matar a Gregorio.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Enamorados, Lucas le propone a Jade fugarse juntos, pero ella piensa en Latiffa, que teme quedarse sin marido. Sidi Alí sospecha que su sobrina le oculta algo y le tiende una trampa.

programme.title: VIP
programme.desc: La vida de Yoo-ri cambia de la noche a la mañana. Jung-sun descubre la identidad de la amante de Sung-joon y luego le muestra a su esposo lo que podría pasar en caso de divorcio.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Jacob pasa la noche con Lea pensando que se trataba de Raquel, ya que sus cuñados pusieron droga en su bebida. Raquel se entera de lo que paso y que por eso su padre la encerró. Esaú golpea brutalmente a Mahalat por hacer daño a Elizaf.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Maine’s North Woods is the largest tract of undeveloped forest land east of the Rocky Mountains. The vast expanse of trees has the ability to offset 500 million metric tons of carbon dioxide from our atmosphere, which is equal to taking a million cars off the road-- for a century! Tonight, Ted Reinstein reports on the New England Forestry Foundation’s efforts to spread the word about the North Woods’ untapped potential to address the climate crisis.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Mount Desert Island is a magnet for hikers, campers and sightseers. The Schoodic Peninsula – not so much. Surprising, because the two places have so much in common, including stunning coastal views and untamed forests. The difference: the Schoodic has no crowds. Tonight, we explore the peninsula, sampling local specialties like pickled wrinkles and dulse, revisit one of our favorite destinations, Wild Blueberry Land, and groove to Flash in the Pans – the hottest steel drum band in the state.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A glimpse of unspoiled Maine for those “from away.” Tonight, Anthony Everett takes us up to Maine’s scenic St. George Peninsula. In Port Clyde, we get the history of a lighthouse saved by local volunteers and a labor of love. In Owls Head, Anthony takes to the skies in a vintage bi-plane and catches a ride in a 1929 Rolls Royce. We sample a taste of Africa in the middle of Rockland at Me Lon Togo, where the chef is also the musical entertainment. Finally, we spend time with a woman who is 102-years-young and still hauling lobsters.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s a milestone year for WCVB (50 years) and Chronicle (40 years)! Tonight we meet others who are marking major milestones this year. The Boston Gay Men’s Chorus is celebrating 40 years on the scene; we talk to original members who recall the early days. Marking 100 years are family-owned businesses George’s Shoes in Jamaica Plain and Lopez the Florist in Dorchester (where Erika Tarantal gets a lesson in flower-arranging). Tune in for those milestone stories and more. Then stay tuned for a prime-time special, WCVB: 50 Years.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes and inside-the-workshop journey of what goes into making music. We discover the precise and time-consuming task of making violins in Boston’s North End. From bass-making in Billerica to ancient Greek instruments in Lowell, we meet artisans and enthusiasts collecting, crafting, and restoring special instruments. We also get a glimpse at how music heals with therapy in Lawrence, and visit a church group in Brookline helping to launch a new generation of Black musicians in Roxbury.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Nicole Estaphan “guides” us through stories all about maps (including that Californian now selling them in Pawtucket, R.I.)

programme.title: LIVE: CityLine
programme.desc: CityLine5 is WCVB's award-winning urban news and feature magazine talk show.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We may not be able to run 26.2 miles, but we can definitely travel that distance in search of good food. Tonight, the Chronicle team is eating our way from Hopkinton to Boston.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We may not be able to run 26.2 miles, but we can definitely travel that distance in search of good food! Tonight, the Chronicle team is eating our way from Hopkinton to Boston.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Scanned the real estate listings lately? Inventory is low, prices are sky-high, and some buyers are going to extreme lengths to lock in a purchase. Tonight Shayna Seymour surveys the market. We meet a Worcester mom who was forced to widen her search to find an affordable home, and families choosing to remodel rather than compete with other buyers. A local home inspector shares a few hints (and horror stories). Finally, Shayna gets a peek inside a $7 million mansion that offers a taste of the celebrity lifestyle.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Water changes from a gray to a blue-green, flowers begin to bloom, and with restaurants reopening it can only mean one thing: it is spring on Martha’s Vineyard. Shayna Seymour takes a stroll near the Edgartown Harbor light, checks in at the Harbor View hotel, visits the Oak Bluffs Gingerbread Houses, and warms up with a bowl of conch chowder. She also tours the Martha’s Vineyard Museum where she learns about the little-known history of Black and Native American whaling captains.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: “Mightier” is a video game designed to help kids regulate their emotions, whose developers hope to treat kids with ADHD or autism. A new portable MRI – “The Swoop” – can make imaging easier for critically-ill patients. Nicole Estaphan meets clinicians, scientists, and tech pros developing and deploying advanced medical therapies -- right here in Massachusetts. She even finds a tech advancement not for humans, but for horses – a new way to treat equine asthma.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan meets a local mom who has defied the odds and is alive 10 years after a diagnosis of Stage 4 lung cancer, thanks in part to targeted gene therapy.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan meets a student/musician from the Berklee College of Music who was diagnosed with a tumor. Nicole also meets a young woman, one of the 1.5 million Americans with lupus, who underwent a groundbreaking new therapy that a local doctor says is a gamechanger. Tonight Nicole brings us these and other stories of patients and caregivers who are making a difference.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As many as 25 million Americans live with rare, undiagnosed conditions. Tonight, Nicole Estaphan meets individuals living with rare diseases – and the clinicians and researchers who are determined to find answers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There is a national mental health crisis among young people, with girls and young women suffering in particular. Tonight one college student shares with Anthony Everett her personal story of struggling with severe depression and anxiety, and time spent in a crowded emergency room. A social worker who struggled to find help for her own daughter’s mental health crisis describes the critical shortage of care providers. Experts from Boston Children’s Hospital discuss the crisis-level need for more treatment options. Finally, we talk to experts who are finding new ways to connect with young people who need help.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take you along to see the lights, hear the music, and spread the joy of the season. Erika Tarantal meets a woman whose full-time job is spreading holiday cheer by wrapping big hotels and office buildings in festive decor.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take you along to see the lights, hear the music, and spread the joy of the season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Some are tiny – and some aren’t even visible to the naked eye. Tonight Erika Tarantal looks at how very small things can have a very big impact on our lives. Erika meets a researcher studying the brains of tiny ants, as well as two friends who co-founded a cosmetics company that uses microscopic algae as a main ingredient. Then, at the MWRA’s Deer Island treatment plant, Erika gets a lesson in how “bugs” (beneficial bacteria) are used to lay waste to…waste.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In honor of Chronicle’s 40th anniversary, tonight we’re bringing you memorable moments from the “Main Streets and Back Roads of New England” – our show’s longest-running feature – and we’ll also hitch a ride in the Old Chevy for memories from “On the Road.” Prep for our Friday prime-time special with this road trip down memory lane.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When it comes to wealth, transportation, property, health care – there are gaps in society. Tonight we talk to people who are working to bridge those gaps: An Ipswich farm provides low-cost access to fresh produce; a local man built an app to help people with physical disabilities navigate public spaces; a Lawrence nonprofit offers grants to businesses whose owners face barriers to capital. A look at the gaps that separate society, and ideas from individuals who are trying to close them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You don’t have to be a baseball lover to enjoy a visit to one of these New England destinations. But if you happen to love minor league baseball, even better! Tonight, Erika Tarantal catches a Portland Sea Dogs game, and checks out what the southern Maine city has to offer outside Hadlock Field – including a restaurant coincidentally called “Papi.”

programme.desc: Wellness Hour or Get Fixed

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Who knew you could explore endless outdoor adventure, a vibrant food and wine scene, crystal blue freshwater, world-class golf, and a charming downtown- all in Northern Michigan? Join host Hannah Cumler as she showcases the best of Traverse City!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There are historic homes, and then there is the house Anthony Everett visits tonight in Plympton – a saltbox Colonial built in 1669, and now up for sale. In Central Mass., we meet a family of Model-T Ford aficionados, and in Boston’s North End we catch up with the stars of a YouTube series exploring neighborhood history.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Walking through the woods one expects to see trees and bugs, but abandoned zoo cages, old cars, and spider gates-- those are unexpected sights on a socially-distant stroll! Call these findings surprising, odd, or even weird, Ted Reinstein discovers many different delights on his walk in Massachusetts woods tonight.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From a duck boat to a “barrier transfer vehicle” to a wildly popular (and odd-looking) style of shoe. Tonight we discover vehicles, equipment, and people in constant motion.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Americans are wild about muffins, and from blueberry to corn to chocolate chip, Nicole Estaphan digs into the delicious breakfast treat. Nicole scales the muffin top with a visit to one of Muffin House Cafe’s six locations. She talks to a food historian who helps trace the origin of muffins, and visits Bisou Sweet, where the muffins act more like donuts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From John Williams to Ruth Pointer to Peter Wolf, the Boston area has been home to incredible music-makers of all genres. Tonight, a look at Chronicle sit-downs with some local musical greats!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Put your breakdancing shoes on because we are celebrating big milestones in the music world. Anthony Everett starts the party in Norwell, where the cymbal company Zildjian is celebrating an astonishing 400 years.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: The Lost Children of Carlisle
programme.desc: A powerful look at the history of Native American boarding schools as told through the stories of tribal members and their surviving family members, who endured an educational system founded on the concept “Kill the Indian in him, and save the man.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: An Insiders view of Boston; Radio Host, Tour Guide and Chef.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: For decades, Tom Randele lived a quiet, happy life in Lynnfield, Mass. It wasn’t until he lay dying of cancer that he revealed his true past to his loving daughter and wife: He was one of Ohio’s most wanted fugitives.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ready for a test of your New England geographical knowledge? Chronicle is taking you on a mystery ride that involves 100 miles of stone walls and a spot that was once part of an entirely different state.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ready for a test of your New England geographical knowledge? Chronicle is taking you on a mystery ride that involves 100 miles of stone walls and a spot that was once part of an entirely different state.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Sharpen your pencils – tonight it’s another Chronicle quiz! This time we’re looking at real estate “reincarnations” – buildings that served one purpose many moons ago…and today have a very different function. From downtown Boston to rural Vermont, our stops include a sandwich shop, a pottery studio, a private home, and a winery that will have you guessing – what did this place used to be? Log onto our social media during the show and help solve the mysteries!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Anthony Everett and Shayna Seymour broadcast live from “The Hub” at the Boston Convention and Exhibition Center, where the NAACP is kicking off its national convention – returning to Boston for the first time in 40 years. Shayna takes a look at this groundbreaking organization and its Boston chapter, which formed at Park Street Church in 1911 and has been working for equal rights ever since. StormTeam 5’s David Williams introduces us to inspiring young people participating in the Convention’s national achievement competition. Those stories and live guest appearances from the BCEC.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from WCVB NewsCenter 5.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Anthony Everett brings us tales from the tiny island of Nantucket, where spring means sprucing up for the influx of summer visitors. He meets up with the island’s first female Harbormaster and climbs to the top of Sconset’s Sankaty Head Light. Plus, where to find island art? You’ll never guess. From the historic cobblestoned main street to the stretch of sand voted one of the best island beaches in the world, spring is in bloom on the Main Streets and Back Roads of Nantucket.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: The Narragansett people called it “Pettaquamscutt” – a six-mile river that flows from North Kingstown to Narragansett. Tonight Ted paddles along this tidal estuary, shares the region’s natural beauty and history, and meets the people who call this area home. He explores the South County Museum, takes in a baseball game with the Ocean State Waves of the New England Collegiate Baseball League, fuels up at a classic clam shack – Monahan’s – and makes a stop at the legendary Allie’s Donuts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, we meet individuals who are working to preserve and revitalize their tribal traditions, hearing from them in their own words – and their own languages.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Here in Massachusetts we don’t need to go far to find mountains, lush farmlands, coastline, and beautiful sandy beaches. From the cliffs at Aquinnah on Martha’s Vineyard, to the Chesterfield Gorge way out west – tonight, Ted Reinstein takes us on a tour of what’s right in our own backyard: the Bay State’s Top Natural Wonders.

programme.title: Heroes On Call
programme.desc: In the 1960s, Americans were dying at shocking rates due to inadequate emergency care. Enter Freedom House Ambulance Service: the first paramedics unit in the U.S., run and staffed by Black men and women. This is the origin story of EMS.
